one_time_description: Purchase price in excess of identifiable assets (goodwill write-off on acquisition)
economic_notes: Nation operates Cumberland Crossing Inn Limited Partnership (51%), Saskatchewan River Business Corporation, Cumberland House Saskatchewan River Limited Partnership. Chief's Island Community Store sells fuel and supplies. Water treatment plant capital project totaling $6,500,000 committed, expected to be fully funded by ISC.
notes: Fiscal year is 2022 (ended March 31, 2022), not 2024. Audit was signed April 5, 2023, over a year after year-end. Large one-time item: purchase price in excess of identifiable assets $2,297,697 (negative goodwill or acquisition write-off). Gain on disposal of capital assets $211,870. Nation is not in compliance with financial covenants on $4,025,000 of loans, reclassified to current. Specific Claim loan of $2,210,971 outstanding. Accumulated surplus note shows discrepancy: statement shows $10,882,838 but Note 16 subtotals to $11,445,169 (likely a presentation difference). Restricted cash includes $4,860,078 capital projects and $2,555,511 CMHC reserves. Two GBEs accounted for under modified equity method: SRBC and CHSRLP. No remuneration schedule included in extracted text.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
High ISC dependency (77.16%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
